evidence: Function naming is consistently snake_case across all modules (filter_and_compare, analyze_non_srp_by_cterm_distance, build_gene_set, etc.)

Column name conventions are inconsistent between modules: analysis/Uniprot_TMD_search.py uses 'Membrane_Domain_Count' (PascalCase_with_underscores), while protein_analysis_utils.py and app/app.py use 'membrane_domain_count' (lowercase) — both reference the same conceptual column
calc_min_cterm_distance (protein_analysis_utils.py) vs calculate_cterm_distance (Uniprot_TMD_search.py) — same operation, inconsistent naming style (abbreviated vs. spelled-out prefix)
analyze_non_srp_by_cterm_distance / analyze_srp_by_cterm_distance follow the same naming pattern, which is consistent within the pair
analysis/analysis_test.py uses the _test suffix pattern but is in analysis/ not tests/ — inconsistent with the project's convention of putting tests under tests/
IDR/nterm_analysis.py and IDR/idr_analysis.py follow the same module naming convention (lowercase with underscores)

strengths: All Python files use consistent snake_case for function and variable names
Module file naming is uniform: lowercase with underscores throughout
analyze_srp_by_cterm_distance and analyze_non_srp_by_cterm_distance are structurally parallel, demonstrating intentional sibling consistency
convention_outlier.issue_character: The main outlier is column name inconsistency — Membrane_Domain_Count vs membrane_domain_count — which is a silent runtime risk, not just a style preference; and the calc_ vs calculate_ prefix divergence for semantically equivalent functions.
convention_outlier.score_rationale: The codebase is largely convention-consistent for code structure and naming. The primary concrete violations are the column name casing discrepancy (Membrane_Domain_Count vs membrane_domain_count) which can cause silent KeyErrors, and the calc_/calculate_ prefix inconsistency which reduces discoverability. These are real but narrow in scope, keeping the score in the upper-middle range.

issues.summary: Column name 'Membrane_Domain_Count' (PascalCase) in Uniprot_TMD_search.py conflicts with 'membrane_domain_count' (lowercase) used elsewhere
issues.suggestion: Standardize to 'membrane_domain_count' (lowercase) throughout; update Uniprot_TMD_search.py to assign that column name when computing it

issues.summary: Equivalent C-terminal distance functions use inconsistent name prefixes: calc_ vs calculate_
issues.suggestion: After consolidating the two functions (see cross_module_architecture finding), use a single consistent name; 'calculate_cterm_distance' is clearer than the abbreviated form

issues.summary: analysis/analysis_test.py uses test-file naming convention but lives outside tests/
evidence: File is named analysis_test.py, matching pytest's discovery pattern (test_*.py or *_test.py)
All other test files are in tests/ (test_protein_analysis_utils.py, test_filter_analysis.py, etc.)
The file is not collected by pytest because it lacks the test_ prefix on its class/function names, but the naming creates confusion
issues.suggestion: Rename to analysis/exploratory_workflow.py or move to notebooks/ to avoid confusion with the tests/ convention

root_causes: Column naming was set by the upstream Uniprot TSV format during early data loading and was not normalized when the shared utils layer was introduced
analysis_test.py predates the tests/ directory convention
